prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_route_element departure_leg_1)
(initialize_route_element arrival_leg_1)
(initialize_route_element vehicle_route_1)
(initialize_route_element vehicle_route_2)
(provisionally_bind_route_element_to_station departure_leg_1 charger_station_1)
(provisionally_bind_route_element_to_station arrival_leg_1 charger_station_2)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(mark_route_element_planned departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(activate_candidate_departure_charger departure_leg_1 candidate_departure_charger_1 connector_requirement_1)
(reserve_connector_for_route_element departure_leg_1 charger_connector_1)
(confirm_departure_leg_feasibility_with_reserved_connector departure_leg_1 candidate_departure_charger_1 charger_connector_1)
(unassign_connector_requirement_from_route_element departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(release_connector_reservation departure_leg_1 charger_connector_1)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element arrival_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(mark_route_element_planned arrival_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(activate_candidate_arrival_charger arrival_leg_1 candidate_arrival_charger_1 connector_requirement_1)
(reserve_connector_for_route_element arrival_leg_1 charger_connector_1)
(confirm_arrival_leg_feasibility_with_reserved_connector arrival_leg_1 candidate_arrival_charger_1 charger_connector_1)
(unassign_connector_requirement_from_route_element arrival_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(release_connector_reservation arrival_leg_1 charger_connector_1)
(compose_charging_stop_candidate departure_leg_1 arrival_leg_1 candidate_departure_charger_1 candidate_arrival_charger_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(schedule_stop_candidate_into_time_slot charging_stop_candidate_1 departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(mark_departure_leg_ready_for_insertion departure_leg_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(mark_arrival_leg_ready_for_insertion arrival_leg_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(assign_time_slot_and_commit_route_element departure_leg_1 time_slot_1 connector_requirement_1)
(commit_insertion_to_departure_leg departure_leg_1 charger_station_1 time_slot_1)
(unassign_connector_requirement_from_route_element departure_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element arrival_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(assign_time_slot_and_commit_route_element arrival_leg_1 time_slot_1 connector_requirement_1)
(commit_insertion_to_arrival_leg arrival_leg_1 charger_station_2 time_slot_1)
(unassign_connector_requirement_from_route_element arrival_leg_1 connector_requirement_1)
(provisionally_bind_route_element_to_station vehicle_route_1 charger_station_3)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element vehicle_route_1 connector_requirement_1)
(mark_route_element_planned vehicle_route_1 connector_requirement_1)
(allocate_charger_model_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 charger_model_1)
(allocate_session_token_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 session_token_1)
(provision_access_permit_for_stop_candidate vehicle_route_1 access_permit_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(pass_vehicle_route_prebinding_checks vehicle_route_1 access_permit_1 charging_stop_candidate_1 connector_requirement_1)
(reserve_connector_for_route_element vehicle_route_1 charger_connector_1)
(complete_adapter_connector_provisioning_without_option_b vehicle_route_1 charger_model_1 charger_connector_1 access_permit_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(bind_driver_session_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 session_token_1 access_permit_1 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(acknowledge_route_and_mark_ready_for_insertion vehicle_route_1)
(assign_time_slot_and_commit_route_element vehicle_route_1 time_slot_1 connector_requirement_1)
(commit_insertion_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 charger_station_3 time_slot_1)
(unassign_connector_requirement_from_route_element vehicle_route_1 connector_requirement_1)
(release_charger_model_from_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 charger_model_1)
(release_session_token_from_vehicle_route vehicle_route_1 session_token_1)
(release_connector_reservation vehicle_route_1 charger_connector_1)
(provisionally_bind_route_element_to_station vehicle_route_2 charger_station_1)
(assign_connector_requirement_to_route_element vehicle_route_2 connector_requirement_1)
(mark_route_element_planned vehicle_route_2 connector_requirement_1)
(allocate_charger_model_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_2 charger_model_1)
(allocate_session_token_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_2 session_token_1)
(provision_access_permit_for_stop_candidate vehicle_route_2 access_permit_2 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(pass_vehicle_route_prebinding_checks vehicle_route_2 access_permit_2 charging_stop_candidate_1 connector_requirement_1)
(reserve_connector_for_route_element vehicle_route_2 charger_connector_1)
(complete_adapter_connector_provisioning_without_option_b vehicle_route_2 charger_model_1 charger_connector_1 access_permit_2 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(bind_driver_session_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_2 session_token_1 access_permit_2 charging_stop_candidate_1)
(acknowledge_route_and_mark_ready_for_insertion vehicle_route_2)
(assign_time_slot_and_commit_route_element vehicle_route_2 time_slot_1 connector_requirement_1)
(commit_insertion_to_vehicle_route vehicle_route_2 charger_station_1 time_slot_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and predicates. The goal is to insert departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, vehicle_route_1, and vehicle_route_2 into the route, meaning (route_element_inserted) for each.


## Examining insertion requirements
- The task involves inserting multiple route elements like departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, vehicle_route_1, and vehicle_route_2 into an existing route.
- Each insertion aims to achieve a state where route_element_inserted is true for all specified elements.
- The complexity arises from managing multiple objects and predicates during the insertion process.

## Examining shared resources  
- Vehicle routes share departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, and charging_stop_candidate_1, but have different access permits and driver preferences.  
- Session_token_1, charger_model_1, and other resources are single instances, suggesting shared usage across routes.  

## Analyzing initialization needs  
- All route elements, including departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, vehicle_route_1, and vehicle_route_2, must be initialized, planned, and bound.  
- The process involves managing shared legs and ensuring all elements achieve the inserted state, resembling a classical planning problem.  

## Checking driver preferences  
- Driver_preference_1 is available initially, bound to vehicle_route_1, while driver_preference_2 exists but isn't marked as available.  
- This could affect actions like applying driver preferences for vehicle_route_2, potentially impacting the planning sequence.

## Exploring provisional binding  
- Route elements, including legs and vehicle routes, need provisional binding to charger stations to proceed.  
- This step, like binding departure_leg_1 to charger_station_1, creates assigned_charger and makes the station unavailable.  

## Checking planning sequence  
- After initialization, route elements require provisional binding, connector assignment, and marking as planned.  
- For legs, actions like reserve_connector or allocate_adapter follow, aiming to process and make them feasible.  

## Evaluating resource constraints  
- Shared resources like connector_req_1 limit simultaneous processing of multiple route elements.  
- Planning and processing legs, such as departure_leg_1, must account for single-instance resources like charger stations.  

## Analyzing leg processing options  
- Legs can be processed by reserving a connector, activating candidate chargers, and confirming feasibility.  
- Alternatively, allocating an adapter makes the leg tentative, later activating it to release the adapter and ensure feasibility.  

## Reviewing vehicle route integration  
- Vehicle routes also need initialization, binding, and processing, similar to legs, to achieve insertion.  
- The process involves composing charging_stop_candidate after legs are processed and feasible, ensuring alignment with route goals.

## Analyzing connector requirement sharing  
- Connector_requirement_1 is a single resource needed by multiple route elements, including departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, vehicle_route_1, and vehicle_route_2.  
- Route elements must be assigned connector_requirement_1 sequentially, processed, then unassigned to allow sharing, as actions like activate require having the connector.  

## Checking station allocation  
- Charger stations (cs1, cs2, cs3) are initially available, with three stations for four route elements needing binding.  
- Provisional binding makes a station unavailable, but commit_insertion later releases it, allowing reuse for sequencing.  

## Exploring connector reservation issues  
- Reserving connector_1 for one route element makes it unavailable, potentially blocking others from processing.  
- The reservation persists through feasibility confirmation and commit_insertion, creating conflicts for shared legs like departure and arrival.

## Examining vehicle route paths  
- Driver preference (dp1) is available and bound for vehicle_route_1 (v1), enabling an alternative path using driver preference for processing.  
- For vehicle_route_2 (v2), driver preference (dp2) is bound but not available, suggesting a different path is needed to meet insertion goals.  

## Analyzing session token constraints  
- Session_token_1 is a single resource, potentially limiting simultaneous processing of vehicle routes.  
- Binding driver sessions to vehicle routes requires session_token_1, impacting sequencing and availability.  

## Reviewing vehicle route readiness process  
- Completing connector binding with session_token_1 moves vehicle_route_1 to "completed," then "acknowledged" and "ready_for_insertion."  
- Assigning a charging profile requires vehicle_route_readiness_completed, but some paths (e.g., no option_a, no option_b) don't set this, creating a potential gap.

## Reviewing vehicle route readiness process  
- Assigning a charging profile or committing insertion requires vehicle_route_readiness_completed, but some paths (e.g., no option_a, no option_b) don't set this, causing potential gaps.  
- Actions like bind_driver_session_and_mark_readiness_option_a, _option_b, or _both set readiness_completed, but no plain action exists for the no-option case.  

## Exploring alternative readiness paths  
- For vehicle routes without options, consider using a driver preference path, like acknowledge_binding_and_mark_route_ready, which sets ready_for_insertion without requiring readiness_completed.  
- Assign_time_slot_and_commit and commit_insertion_to_vehicle_route can follow, releasing charger stations and time slots after insertion, fitting this path.  

## Analyzing resource availability  
- Driver_preference_1 is available for vehicle_route_1 but not for vehicle_route_2, limiting the driver preference path for vehicle_route_2.  
- Charger stations (cs1, cs2, cs3) are initially allocated, but binding them provisionally may leave vehicle_route_2 without a station, requiring sequencing for releases.  

## Outlining a feasible plan  
- Process departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, vehicle_route_1, and vehicle_route_2 separately, binding stations like cs1 for dl1, cs2 for al1, and cs3 for vr1 initially.  
- For vehicle_route_2, wait for station release (e.g., after committing insertion) to bind and proceed, ensuring shared charger station composition and scheduling.

## Examining vehicle route 1 steps  
- Assigned time slot and committed for vehicle_route_1 using ts1 and cr1, marking it as committed.  
- Committed insertion for vehicle_route_1 released cs1 and ts1, making them available again.  

## Assessing vehicle route 2 constraints  
- Driver_preference_2 is unavailable initially, limiting options for vehicle_route_2 compared to vehicle_route_1.  
- Cannot apply driver preference for vehicle_route_2 due to the lack of available driver preference, requiring an alternative path.  

## Exploring readiness for vehicle route 2  
- Used prebinding path for vehicle_route_2, provisioning access permit and completing adapter connector provisioning without option_b.  
- Marked vehicle_route_2 as prebinding_ready and provisioning_complete, aligning with composed and scheduled csc.  

## Analyzing session token availability  
- Session_token_1 is bound to vehicle_route_1 after its driver preference path, and no automatic release occurs, blocking allocation to vehicle_route_2.  
- Need to explore releasing session_token_1 from vehicle_route_1 or adjusting the order to prioritize vehicle_route_2, as current setup limits sharing.

## Detailing vehicle route steps
- Initialize vehicle_route_X, then provisionally bind it to available charger station csX.
- Assign charger requirement cr1 and mark vehicle_route_X as planned, followed by allocating charger_model cm1.

## Managing session token allocation
- Allocate session_token st1 early after marking planned, binding it to vehicle_route_X for later use.
- Ensure session_token is available before binding, releasing it after insertion for subsequent vehicle routes.

## Completing provisioning process
- Reserve connector cc1 and complete adapter connector provisioning without option_b, marking vehicle_route_X as provisioning_complete.
- Bind driver session to vehicle_route_X using st1, then acknowledge and mark ready for insertion, skipping charging profile assignment.

## Evaluating time window and options
- Note that the path doesn’t require binding time window profile or applying option_a, aligning with no stop_candidate_option_a or option_b.
- Confirm charging_stop_candidate_composed and stop_candidate_scheduled are met for provision_access_permit, ensuring process continuity.

## Managing route element insertion  
- Composed charging_stop_candidate_1 using departure_leg_1, arrival_leg_1, candidate_departure_charger_1, and candidate_arrival_charger_1.  
- Assigned and unassigned connector_requirement_1 between departure_leg_1 and arrival_leg_1 to schedule and insert, ensuring resource availability.  

## Preparing legs for insertion  
- Marked departure_leg_1 and arrival_leg_1 ready for insertion after scheduling charging_stop_candidate_1.  
- Inserted departure_leg_1 and arrival_leg_1 into charger_stations_1 and 2, respectively, releasing time_slot_1 and connector_requirement_1 for reuse.  

## Initiating vehicle route 1 setup  
- Provisionally bound vehicle_route_1 to available charger_station_3 and assigned connector_requirement_1 after freeing it from arrival_leg_1.  
- Marked vehicle_route_1 as planned, allocated charger_model_1 and session_token_1, and completed provisioning without option_b.  

## Finalizing vehicle route 1 insertion  
- Bound driver session to vehicle_route_1 using session_token_1, acknowledged, and marked ready for insertion.  
- Inserted vehicle_route_1 into charger_station_3, releasing resources like time_slot_1 for subsequent vehicle routes.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
